channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: ARE YOU FUCKING KIDDING ME WITH THIS SHIT STOP SELLING HANTA IS A NOTHING BURGER 40% MORTALITY JUST MEANS THEY BURN OUT… — 56 replies — 224.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
ARE YOU FUCKING KIDDING ME WITH THIS SHIT STOP SELLING HANTA IS A NOTHING BURGER 40% MORTALITY JUST MEANS THEY BURN OUT…

Open thread • Replies: 56 • Updated: 2026-05-08 11:59 UTC

OP

ARE YOU FUCKING KIDDING ME WITH THIS SHIT
STOP SELLING
HANTA IS A NOTHING BURGER
40% MORTALITY JUST MEANS THEY BURN OUT BEFORE SPREADING FUCK
I LITERALLY JUST BOUGHT BACK

Early replies (first 3)

62202000
Fuck off retard

62202033
>>62201978
>“it is possible more cases will be reported” because it can be up to six weeks before those infected with the Andes variant of hantavirus show symptoms.

Lmao 40% mortality with SIX WEEKS to transmit it would fucking DESTROY humanity if it's even mildly transmissible without symptoms.

62202089
>>62202033
Bullish on Hanta? Truly a nothing burger though, this disease is likely less transmissible than herpes.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62203775
>>62203771
[spoiler]
you already have
[/spoiler]

62203787
>>62202774
that's because china censorship DUN GOOFED us.
now the virus is going to places with freedom of the press.

62203799
>Viruses exist

Is someone going to tell him that germs are just a THEORY as well?

62203831
>>62202196
they are plaguemaxxing

62204048
>>62201978
Viruses are not real niggers. Do you even 4chan?

62204078
>>62202144
Cant even afford to live in my city. Homes go for 3mm.

62204346
>>62203234
Yeah we have limited info but if the transmission window is long enough especially when someone infected is without symptoms or minimal symptoms and easy enough we're fucked.

I would begin buying up N100 masks as well as powerful UV C bulbs and all the other shit you wished you had during COVID NOW. It could be a nothingburger but I'd be ready.

62204414
>>62204078
Nigger you think I only have $3MM to my name?

62204452
I fucking hate waging so I welcome another lockdown.

62204459
>>62201978
Do you think a second lockdown scenario would be highly bullish for crypto like the last one was?

62204460
>>62204459
Yes.

Here's the play:
>OTM puts the moment you see second degree transmission (patient zero transmitted to person who transmitted to someone else)
>SELL as SOON as the President is about to speak
>All into crypto (BTC/XMR)

62204470
>>62204346
This isn't like Covid in one very important way - we have known about Hantavirus for a half century now (it was discovered during the Korean war), and we know about this specific south american strain of hanta.
The transmission window is extremely short, less than a day, and it's blatantly obvious when you're potentially infectious because you're running an extremely high fever. Also, even during that period of peak infectiousness it's still not terribly transmissible, most hanta virus outbreaks involve just a few people, and most of those turn out to have gotten it directly from rodent droppings. The outbreak in arizona back in the 90's for example came from infected rodents in a warehouse that stored canned sodas, and the dust from their droppings was getting onto the top of the cans and people were getting infected from drinking the soda. It looked like person-to-person transmission within families until they figured out it was coming from the soda cans and everybody in the family was drinking the same sodas.

62204475
>>62202000

Fpbp

62204543
>>62201978
>Load ze Hantavirus FUD

62204585
>>62201978
The virus has between a 2 week and 8 week incubation time.

It spreads by aerosol droplets.

The virus can remain virulent within a droplet for between 2 and 15 days.

It has a 40% rate of mortality.

This is not 'we treat you with a vaccine' shit. This is 'we shoot you and burn the body' shit.

You won't hear about the billionaires running into their bunkers. They'll be there long before things get hairy.

62204672
>>62204585
Nah. This is another hoax to get everyone to inject another mark of the beast into their arm. Fuck that. So fucking obvious and they aint fooling anybody after the 1st round.

62204681
>>62204672
Get the jab, schizo! You are killing people!!!

62204692
>>62204681
Fuck off. I took the covid vax and im not taking another

62204874
Whats with the nonstop pandemics? The last one was just 6 years ago. Why we getting literal viral plague now?

62204956
>>62204672
there's no rule that they have to do the same thing twice. It could be different.

62204967
>>62204874
Israel's pr department are desperate to change the topic

62204988
>>62204967
I suppose this is it. Another nothing burger vax forced upon me

62205070
>>62202033
>if it's even mildly transmissible without symptoms.
It's not transmissible while asymptomatic, it's extremely hard to catch it while symptomatic (onset is like the flu so it can happen, but requires a lot of fluids and isn't aerosolized).

If you actually want to doomsay then the real issues is if it gets into rodent populations then it's just a recurring issue.

62205228
>>62205070
Then kill the rodents whats the big deal?

62205278
Stop falling for /pol/ bot threads. No one else cares about it at all

62205340
>>62202909
So like a one week timeframe they changed to possible human transmission? Just like this thing lol

62205341
>>62205278
Good point desu

62205352
humanity exists for 300000 years and now all of a sudden we have global humanity ending pandemics back to back within just a few years. yeah this seems a LITTLE bit unlikely and no I wont stop noticing.

62205358
>>62205340
Correct, they only have so much information so "the story" will change the moment they get more.

There's nothing wrong with that besides being a retard who thinks that since something is "the facts" all other possibilities have been "deboonked".

If you have an IQ over 110 you should be able to see the situation as one of many possibilities of varying probabilities, and update your weightings as more information becomes available.

Again, highly likely it won't transmit well enough but keep your eyes peeled for new cases.

item.title: I’ve been without work now for a few weeks and this is the saddest state of existance i’ve ever been in. — 62 replies — 222.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
I’ve been without work now for a few weeks and this is the saddest state of existance i’ve ever been in.

Open thread • Replies: 62 • Updated: 2026-05-08 11:55 UTC

OP

I’ve been without work now for a few weeks and this is the saddest state of existance i’ve ever been in. I’m just completely alone in my apartment with nothing important to do, lonely. I might as well not exist at all. It’s like i’m the biggest loser in high school or something. The people here who advocate for the NEET lifestyle must be some real freaks.

Early replies (first 3)

62201436
I am truly sorry to hear that you dont have anything meaningful to do in your life or have an identity besides being a wageslave. This is entirely a (you) problem

62201447
Apartment? Let me guess youre living in some shitty city surrounded by brown people in some shitty apartment and youre wondering why your life is shit. You need to fix your environment, you do not need to hypnotize yourself by working some bullshit job that you dont care about

62201460
>>62201428
I'm sorry to say that you've been brainwashed. If you can't do anything but work then you should kill yourself. Actually don't because we need people like you to do "important things" like putting our fries in the bag

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62202075
>>62201969
>feels meaningless
Probably so apparent from your first phrase. Stuck in head, ego, I bet you think *you* have *your thoughts* right? in *your* mind?
The illusion becomes easier to escape with practice.

62202092
>>62202019
>it will finally have it’s world dominating empire in the form of EU
Do you actually believe the nonsense Brussels tells the world 24/7 in utter disregard for reality?
That's almost as bad as trusting the Kremlin even a single word.
If I had to bet on who will lead the world a few years down the line, it will be countries that haven't attempted to kill their economy (and by extension their people) for absolutely insane ideological reasons, while suppressing any and all opposition.
So probably the US, Argentine, Japan and whoever else isn't fucked in the head. The US obviously only in case the Democratic Party doesn't burn it to the ground (in "mostly peaceful protest"!), because Americans keep voting for "Anti-Democrats".

62202094
>>62202055
>>62202071
Damn chuds are retarded

62202108
>>62202092
I see, everyone advocating for neetdom really has a two digit iq. Brussels isn’t in control of what people post on twitter and tiktok lmao. They aren’t some kind of autocracy.

62202135
>>62202108
You don't even follow who you are replying to, despite /biz/ having IDs.
I don't know what the other guys have posted and I am also not interested in it, but the retard is probably (You).

62202208
>>62201436
fpbp and
/thread

62202281
Read Books
Learn to juggle
go hang out in a Park
Make Mix tapes (Playlists)
write some poetry
Look for weird junk at a thrift store
Ride the Bus until last stop, check it out and ride it all the way home
Go for a swim
Fly a kite, or better yet, design and build one, then fly it
Walk around the cool area of your city with all the exposed brick and enjoy a nice IPA

I hung out in the park for a whole shift (8hrs) the other day, it was great. I bought some snacks, a book and kendama.

62202328
>>62202019
LARP. Prove you’re a doctor then. If you can’t then I will assume you’re a do nothing email/spreadsheet monkey. The fact you’re replying to everyone nearly immediately proves to me that you don’t have an important job at all

62202375
>>62202328
Yeah maybe i do hold people in this place in too high regard.

62202431
>>62201436

62202479
>>62202375
Lmao. So you’re not a doctor then. Why LARP on this shithole? I’m legitimately curious

62202484
>>62201428
go to the gym

62202485
>>62201428
>why do you try to avoid public settings

62202492
>>62201428
I've been studying things that might be useful
>accounting
>python
>graphic design
>shoveling coal
>moving blocks of ice
basically obsolete shit, it keeps me busy

62202512
>>62202485
I think you have to an hero by cop in that situation

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JXtoio7Ivhk

62202878
>>62202479
I’m a doctor, i said nothing about that, just that i hold people here in too high regard.

62202886
>>62201588
You sound either retarded or clinically depressed. Consult a psychiatrist

62202894
>>62202886
I’ve worked as a psychiatrist. How so?

62203008
>>62201969
>Oh noooo men can’t force with violence women into breeding with men they don’t find attractive anymore!
only low T men think this is a good thing

62203201
>>62203008
High T men don't even have to try to get women to sleep with them. Low T, ugly, poor, beta faggots are the ones that need to force women, kek.

62203328
It depends on your personality and other factors. For me I'm able to handle it usually but now I'm sick and it prevents me from doing the activities I liked to do as a neet. Feels like I'm waiting to die but it was relatively fun while it lasted. Too bad money couldn't save me in the end

62203501
>>62202019
Oh no its the insufferable doctor again that likely to discuss endlessly and is high on his intelligence but a typical midwit as most doctors.
>>62201969
>Oh noooo men can’t force with violence women into breeding with men they don’t find attractive anymore! Only low value men care about that. Do only low value men like neeting?
Classical strawman. Its not about forcing to breend. Its about changing incentives back to normal without propaganda and support from the government. Also its all about your face, not your "value". You would understand if you were not handsome.
>>62202041
Bullshit. These are 3 of 1000s of historical systems. And even these, britain was 100% based on race. rome too (barbarians) and the persians had words for "non aryans". So literally 100% wrong.
>>62201966
Vielleicht helfen die neuen Maßnahmen, mehr Geld für Rentner und Beamte und höhere Steuern. XD
Aber im Ernst es wird nur ein langsamer Abstieg. Zusammenbruch gibts nur bei Krieg oder sowas.

62203529
>>62203501
>Also its all about your face, not your "value". You would understand if you were not handsome.
Wrong. I should have said mostly, or its the basis. Of course status and money and character matter.

62203638
>>62201588
You kind of sound like a faggot

62204797
>>62203501
There were a lot of romaniced barbarians in the romam empire and they could work in high positions in rome. There are huge amount of multicultural empires in history and once again we’re all results of different populstions mixing. The german idea of a pure race is another too simple and short sighted ideology from back when people didn’t know just how much we are results of different populations mixing over time. Even germany isn’t one race but had celtic mixture in the south and slavic on the east. Britain was celtic and three different kinds of germanic. I’m not saying go full blanda upp but migration and multiculturalism has always existed. Sure you we can try to make the flow lesser but completely ending it is a pipe dream. And why should we? There are a lot of good immigrants. All in all it’s no excuse to think ”society is shit” unless you got mind broken by /pol/-propaganda.

62204835
>>62201428
Ahh goycattle Stockholm Syndrome. Have you read about the freed slaves after the Civil War who killed themselves?

62204853
The conclusion I have come to is that 99% of /biz/ is depressed and/or facing existential crisis.

62204867
>>62201428
The bad news: You are a brainless golem that can't do anything unless he's commanded to by someone else.
The good news: You're in good company, since this is most of humanity.

Now just go get another wagejob, wagegolem.

62205336
>>62204853
Sounds like it. Would be nice to have this discussion brought over to /lit/.

62205354
>>62201588
> without any reason for it
This is such short-term thinking. You may have a problem with instant gratification.
What’d you lose your job for and why are you shitposting about NEETs inserts of asking for advice on getting a job?

item.title: The only IQ test that matters is whether you're smart enough to find a way out of waging, whether it's building and sell… — 32 replies — 128.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
The only IQ test that matters is whether you're smart enough to find a way out of waging, whether it's building and sell…

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-05-08 12:11 UTC

OP

The only IQ test that matters is whether you're smart enough to find a way out of waging, whether it's building and selling a business, investing in the right assets, or getting rich from online content.Everything else is irrelevant.

Early replies (first 3)

62204408
having a job again would be pretty comfy though
I would even go into the office

62204412
pretty much, yea

62204418
Got a call for a job for which I have the certs about remote management. But they called 6 hours ago, I was going to call first thing in the morning so lol they probably won't even asnwer.

But in the 1% chance I get them on the line, force the recruiter to TALK ABOUT THAT JOB and refuse to send over my resume unless considered for that specific job and can get that job, the first step in my plan to work 3 remote jobs will be complete.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62204420
What if you're smart enough to find a job that you enjoy. Or even just smart enough to gaslight yourself into enjoying waging. Ulyimately, the goal is enjoying life, it doesn't really matter how you get there.

62204428
>>62204418
ello sar,
I just need the last 4 digits of your social security number and your DOB month and day only for unique numbers in our candidate tracking system. please sir. this is our process. we must follow or else you cannot apply

62204447
>>62204400
>>62204420
the problem is that labor is one of the most expensive parts of running a business. so when tough times come and the company needs to cut costs, labor is the first to go. to minimize your chance of being let go you have to bring value to the company and stay at the top of your field. this applies or the rest of the labor force. the top 10% of any field are the ones that get offers. so unless you are in a field you dominate in, being in the labor force will always have an element of uncertainty. not to mention the fed itself likes a certain potion of the population unemployed to cool off inflation. employers also benefit from having people desperate because they can be picky. all this to say that i agree with OP. fuck these jobs. the moment i make it i will never work a job again.

62204458
>>62204400
Einstein was a genius and rich (like $12MM net worth today) but he did what he loved and worked till he died. So that kind of throws what you're saying out the window, doesn't it

62204485
>>62204458

>Einstein
>Genius

This retard stole his wife’s work and couldn’t even write. Lmao

62204980
>>62204400
I 100% agree with this. The internet and technology has made this the easiest time to become rich ever just by virtue of the wealth of information at everybody's fingertips but the idiots use it just to complain about how hard they have it instead of trying literally anything to improve their situation.
I have offered to teach family members about how I made it (with stocks and option spreads) but they would not even try to learn. They just wanted to give me money and have me invest it for them.
I lost the last shred of sympathy I had for normies it would have been so easy to scam them out of all their hard wagied life-savings and they would have absolutely deserved it.

62204991
>>62204458
being a tenured professor with his reputation wasn't really a job but more of a playground for him to do whatever he wanted.

62204998
people will be like yeah im high iq then be a poor pathetic loser working some pleb brainlet job lmao

62205005
>>62204980
>I have offered to teach family members about how I made it (with stocks and option spreads) but they would not even try to learn. They just wanted to give me money and have me invest it for them.

assuming youre really successful and not LARPing, your family and friends are right to prefer you do it for them. it requires skills and a mentality that cant be taught. you must know theres many people that know more than you that have failed in the markets

62205044
>>62205005
I partially agree and disagree. I concur that while there are a myriad ways to make it like OP said there is a sort of "emotional detachment" mentality that will be immensely beneficial in all of them. While this may come easier to some people than others I absolutely refuse to believe that this state of mind and the associated skills cannot be taught. I see it merely as a matter of developing a habit, similar to regular working out or learning an instrument.
The most fundamental of all these habits if one of continuous learning. I tried pennystocks, daytrading, TA and various unlawful enterprises in order to make it before I found a niche in which I could consistently beat the market and I am still studying new strategies in an effort to diversify.
They however did not even want to start. It would have been perfectly fine if my investment strategies would have been too stressful or risky for their tastes but they never even engaged. They just expect someone else to do things while they watch TV and bitch about the current thing.

In one of the MITs courses they put on youtube about the service itself the professor stated that when universities started putting their lectures online for free they expected that underachieving/underprivileged students would get a leg up from them but instead the only ones who used them at all where already the top students/overachievers.
I have started to see the K-shape everywhere.

62205048
I can think of a lot of really smart engineers who would never be able to run a business to save their lives. I don't think of them as idiots.
Though they could probably hack it as quant traders if they felt like it, go independent that way.

62205049
>>62204400
>have to work to do any of that shit
can't escape
even investing in the right assets has psychological work. reassuring yourself you picked correctly...

62205173
>>62205044
>ways to make it
no such thing

62205197
>>62204980
Teach me, I'm willing to learn.

I understand the risks of options.

62205235
>>62205197
test

62205240
>>62204458
being an elite college professor isn't a real job. you aren't expected to teach, unless you want to. you're given grants gibs left and right to investigate whatever ideas you want. it's like being nobility.

62205246
>>62204980
>Just do something you don't want to do bro!!

It's as hard for me to invest in stocks or do YouTube or any of that boring shit as it would be to stack shelves or whatever. Arguably setting up a business or anything is 100x more boring and difficult than just stacking shelves

62205252
>>62204400
agree too. I managed to get my NW up to about $2 million,I only made it so far because of crypto, I never treated it like real money until I cashed out. I need around 2.8 to comfortably retire but I am such a pussy, I cannot take big risks, I am just too afraid. My plan is to put about 500k into some triple leveraged ETF the next time we get a bigger pullback. pls halp me make it as fast as possible I cannot tolerate waging anymore.

62205253
>>62204400
>The only IQ test that matters is
The #1 answer to this is experience of the Divine. This being the highest state of Knowing Thyself.

A distant #2 is being able to order one's obligation & success in the material world.

t. cagie hater

62205255
>>62205197
>>62205235
Ah shit my post didn't go through so I'll try again

Look up these terms first:
>Calls
>Puts
>Nakes/Covered
>Assignment
>Premium
>The greeks
>IV
>Maximum Pain
>Calender Spreads
>Rolling (the Wheel)

Stay within the risk you are comfortable with. Don't chase Premiums, it will get you burnt. Buy to close options you sold instead of letting them expire. Even if they expire worthless you will give up the time in the weekend if you don't roll them over.
Ex-Dividend Dates and earnings will greatly increase the chances of assignment, plan accordingly.

NEVER EVER SELL NAKED SHORTS!!! YOU WILL END UP LIKE MELVIN CAPITAL!

If you sell calls above your cost basis and puts below you will always make (some) money. If one end goes in the money you can roll them up or down to the nearest higher/lower price that will give you more premium than the buy to close price. This will give you more money and allow you to catch a potential move back to the mean, while rolling to the nearest out of the money price might leave you dry for months.

Some good resources here
https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLOweupE79XXiBaeH_xBpkUcYUsrAaKQen
https://www.youtube.com/@BradFinn/videos

thetagang.com/
maximum-pain.com/

62205271
you guys hate waging because you have shitty entry level nigger tier humiliating jobs

62205275
>>62205271
I got a good job and after a couple of years even that becomes tedious. I do think it makes you appreciate time off more though

62205279
>>62205271
If you can't quit this job that you love tomorrow if you wanted to then you're still entry level at being in control of your life.

62205283
>>62205271
I got full WFH engineering job, I can do whatever I want I still hate it.

62205334
>>62204400
>be me
>never worked a day in my life
>the IQ move was to simply force the government to pay me a monthly wage for being mentally ill

62205339
>>62205253
This, finding God is real iq test. Anything else in life is a sidequest

62205388
>>62205173
Anyone 300+ living paycheck-to-paycheck is stupid. I don’t care if they’re a coastie, get a roommate like other people have to

62205395
>>62205275
>>62205279
>>62205283
Just put the fries in the bag

62205397
>>62204400
If you weren't part of the top 10% in 2025, you're lower class

item.title: How do LINK niggers cope with the fact that they were bag holding for 6, but I repeat, SIX years and have nothing to sho… — 6 replies — 17.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
How do LINK niggers cope with the fact that they were bag holding for 6, but I repeat, SIX years and have nothing to sho…

Open thread • Replies: 6 • Updated: 2026-05-08 11:51 UTC

OP

How do LINK niggers cope with the fact that they were bag holding for 6, but I repeat, SIX years and have nothing to show for it?

Early replies (first 3)

62205213
I just keep moving forward

62205218
>>62205209
Literally no one has been holding this entire time, people buy when it is low and sell when it is high, rinse and repeat.
The hodl meme is not a thing.

62205224
yikes

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62205225
>>62205209
Not being a histrionic little bitch helps, a lot. It sometimes goes up, it sometimes goes down, and it always goes sideways one way or the other. Just like my other investments. Why fret?

I know I know, it must be hard to grasp as someone with unregulated emotions and frequent bouts of selfhate and loathing, but you too can learn to not give a fuck anon. Just work on yourself and your career.

62205264
>>62205225
>if you keep clinging on to a dead alt for almost a decade without ever re-evaluating the original thesis, you're "patient", otherwise you're a histrionic bitch!!!1
ngl impressive mental gymnastics

62205343
>>62205225
>it sometimes goes up, it sometimes goes down
Sometimes it's also, you know, fucking grows a little and not just goes back to levels it was at 6 years ago.
I know I know, it must be hard to cope as someone who holds LINK for years just to see Sirgay dump another fat stack of coins onto the market making every single baggie pay for it. But hey at least linkies get to work on themselves and their career, am I right? It's not like they have any other choice lmao.

item.title: The LINK token has value — 136 replies — 315.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
The LINK token has value

Open thread • Replies: 136 • Updated: 2026-05-08 11:46 UTC

OP

The LINK token has value

Early replies (first 3)

62176216
New Chainlink report
https://bitwiseinvestments.com/crypto-market-insights/chainlink-in-plain-english

62176226
>>62176214
>In many cases, nodes must stake LINK before they can provide and be compensated for their services. If they provide incorrect data, they are penalized and lose a portion of the LINK they have staked. This system helps ensure the integrity of Chainlink’s services while also
reducing the free-floating supply of LINK.
Speaking in present tense. I assume this and the and the other clues we've seen in the past couple weeks mean staking imminent?

62176244
>>62176226
What other clues

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62201004
>>62199326
nice chart cuckold. Check the price :)

62201058
HOLY SHIT

By sheer chance I happened to be watching Sunny today, "The gang finds a dumpster baby", and recognised one of the frames Asslarper used (pic related).

In this episode, Dennis (AB's avatar) convinces an activist to CHAINLINK himself to a tree, then LITERALLY cuckolds him.

>A SINGULAR TREE
>THE cuckOLDS OF CRYPTO!!!

Now do you believe me?

Inverse Assblaster; what did he tell you was useless?

62202072
>>62199117
>guy with no gains in half a decade writes a psych 101 paper rationalizing why people are discussing something on a discussion board

>duuuuuuuuuuude just donate and don't ask questions

>worships a "god" with a fake beard who pretends to know about banking


THE Cuckolds of crypto

62202448
>>62200993
I wonder how much ccip fees will go up by due to this >>62198190

62202542
>>62195762
what is the point of your reply when you obviously cannot provide any proof to the fabled off-chain revenue
>>62199117
notice how these shills never actually address the "fud" and simply dismiss everything at once
that anon is right>>62199240, this is literally XRP 2.0

62202580
>>62200039
>Nobody's shilling you link
meanwhile, this guy>>62199896 has made hundreds of posts during the past few days, aggresively shilling and pushing bullish narratives
the gaslighting is truly insane

62202717
>>62176214
It's over bros, our Chainlink journey ends here. The Great Reset timeline has come at a crossroad with humanity. The magic is gone, the memes are stale, the shills have been uncovered, the agendas laid bare for all to see, i-i-it's over marines...only tears from here.

62203105
>>62200993
1,5k in 7 days. Exceptional product/revenue stream. 1 staked LINK = 1$ per month amirite?

62203137
>>62203105
Quite funny considering xrp does a big fat 0 in revenue lmao

62204653
>>62201058
You forgot the picture

62204670
>>62202542

to point out that businesses don't usually disclose a full list of private transactions
the proof is the chainlink reserve, the partnerships, you don't need exact numbers
>>62202580

>>62203105

>>62203137
as soon as xrp makes revenue the speculation is gone, it's always pre-revenue

62204675

"here is a far too long clip of sergey saying the same thing he said 9 years ago when i first gave him my money, do you like it?" "yes i like it, we are going to be very rich" "agreed, i attached the grainy 9 year old picture to my post" stuff like that

62204715

62204726

62204731

They’re basically the uber of crypto. Uber sucks balls, I’d rather pay my driver directly for the full price

>>62204731

it secures all the others and connects them to the real world. you can't run many useful smart contracts without it

62204983

62205169

62205182

62205203

62205204

62205259

item.title: No one is talking about how there's no jobs. — 89 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
No one is talking about how there's no jobs.

Open thread • Replies: 89 • Updated: 2026-05-08 01:08 UTC

OP

No one is talking about how there's no jobs. Ive been working for 10 years and never seen the market nearly this fucked up. Every job is fake, they will interview for 3 or 4 rounds and still repost the job next week, after ghosting people of course. People irl are coping so hard. Im back for my masters and not one person has any job or internship in any of my classes this or previous semester except me(and I will get laid off soon). These zoomers are so fucking deluded. They cant get an internship so they say "of well, im just gonna focus on finishing my masters". Is this how THEY are getting away with it? Society has already brainwashed people so badly the people in charge can drink their milkshake and leave them destitute and they wont even notice?

Early replies (first 3)

62184289
>>62184286
skill issue. ggf &c

62184301
>>62184289
All these interviews just happened to not like me or anyone else they interviewed and reposted the job? What a coincidence. Crazy how i had skills for the past 10 years and always instantly found a job but something randomly happened and now I lost whatever skills I have as I got good excperince in my fields

62184315
>>62184301
Oh and here is the email from my on-site interview on may 4th i got on friday. Nevermind it was my drram company and this tore my fucking hear out. Skill issue on my part that they canceled the role..

Oh and this is only 2/3 of all my interviews this past months if u count phone calls

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62188591
>>62184289
>skill issue.
reddit post.

62188606
>>62184286
>they will interview for 3 or 4 rounds
By the way, they totally could figure it out in one interview. But they string you along for 3+ interviews because they think it's funny.

62188643
>>62184286
Just learn to prompt

62188765
>>62184556
>Reality is an IQ test, what do you think your IQ is?
So my boss must be smarter than me...that's why im retiring in 3 years and that fucker will be slaving until 80

62188796
>>62186790
Which is why those ideologies are either dumb or evil tempting.

62188815
>>62188279
It’s funny how you mix Ukraine with some jewish conspiracy.

62188832
>>62188796
No, communism is evil and everyone who promotes communism is a soulless monster.

62188926
>>62188815
>>62188832
Israeli IP

62188963
you laughed at le trade meme
hahhahahah
I can work anywhere I want, more jobs then I can shake a stick at, getting phone calls about asking if Im available for dispatch, sorry currently employed!

62190507
>>62185702
That all just sounds like bullshit

>>62187055
Huh? If the count was based on people who want a job why are you talking about slavery

62190516
>>62185827
>>62188258
Those are just an obnoxious type of ghost listing
https://www.fastcompany.com/91019872/employers-are-intentionally-leaving-job-openings-unfilled-heres-why

62190529
capitalism doesn't work
king now
kill jew
sell orc inc mik wop feminists
force abourt preg not want
free slave girls n rooms for white male engineer, everyone else 0 money
red lights gone instead put road circle small
highway have middle u turn lanes, no overpass
flibe.com free power
greenarrays.com replaces computing university school lawyer movies games net etc.
more barges n canals
monorails
trains
etc
dymaxion car burning hydrogen in stirling engine exhaust water
dam huge waterway for hydroelectric
100% white west playground :)
king all the way

62192053
>>62188815
kys shill-rat.
>>62188832
agreed, thats why i hate kikes, they invented communism in their kike-think-tanks, weaponised it and unleashed it on Russia+china, and the world. Kikes are either directly, or indirectly, behind 95% of all human suffering for the last ~200 years.

62194013
>>62184286
Everyone is talking about that - on plebbit and normie sites. This board is for retards with $3000 saved to gamble it on shitcoins dreaming of becoming a millionaire.

62194027
>>62185045
>network engineer
>CompTIA
Doubt. Even a CCNA won't get you a job.
CompTIA is basically worthless.

62194055
>>62184286
>Ive been working for 10 years

Woah, woah, woah Hold up there gramps.

I haven't seen attack ships on fire off the shoulder of Orion, but I am getting video interviews at least. The problem is usually the company will cuck out at the last minute and cancel the req before I get a chance to start. I've been between jobs now for 8 months, almost 10% of your career, and it happens quite a bit. I suggest you pick up a book. Maybe pass the 3 CFA exams. Until everyone learns to stop being faggots about AI, we're in for a bumpy ride because galaxy brains like yourself think you can automate and forget it. Some businesses will have to get fucked up first THEN they can start hiring humans again

62194083
>>62188643
this is serious

>>62188963
you'll be poorer than you ever imagined soon

>>62190529
this. I would like to subscribe thank you

62194913
>>62194027
even tho the faggot you replied to was replying to me, I believe his story. I joined a contractor IT job and they had many clients whom I got to interact with. Every single one of them liked me and boasted about the work i did. I reached out to the one i liked most when I was ready to rejoin employment after a year break and they swooped me up with no interview or credential checking. I do not have any certs, dropped out of college since it was a meme and a scam and learned everything through projects I was doing based from my hobbies (gaming).
>certs, degrees, etc are useless in clown world
>proof of work always works, like BTC if your proof of work is amazing YOU WILL BE SOUGHT OUT
>after a year of interacting with AIs, i asked them to give me an IQ estimate based upon our interactions and all of them told me 135+
While you are worrying about wasting time "meeting" their requirements for work, employers are wasting time meeting my requirements to work for them. You and I are not the same

62197152
>>62184413
Try saying that in public to 1/4 of the population and see what happens.

62200034
>>62184286
there's a looooot of boomers still in the workforce, and the news now says they have more wealth than all other generations combined.

62200057
>>62185888
I decided I wanted to go to law school and then was brutally denied with a 167 while negresses who complied they only had a 151 were accepted. Not to good schools, mind you, but seeing some 30yo fat black chick making 20k a month in DC and complaining she can't make ends meet and finding out she only got a 155 on the LSAT but went to Harvard Law is just a massive FUCK you.

62200063
>>62186171
ironic since Shrub is the reason things got so bad.

>>62186790
Capitalism and eugenics are inherently at odds, social darwinism means I'm smart enough to bash you in with a rock while you cry as I execute your kids gang style. Capitalism is your daddy got you a nice job and you think you're a tough guy. These are at opposite ends of the value spectrum.

62200068
>>62194913
networking doesn't matter when you get your head bashed in with a rock.

62200076
>>62200034
there's a 75yo at the local mail room who glares at me when I come in, but is like sugar for every 20-30yo woman. He's a lazy fuck and a creep. These guys are one bad day from keeling over and they're still competing with us for women, our fathers/grandfathers, like it's 1975. I hate them so bad.

62200088
>>62200057
Fuck, really? What was your GPA?

62200155
>>62197152
Most people have a job NEETcel.

62200169
>>62200155
You do know that they've been consistently revising those numbers way down right? I have a job currently myself, but I know Jerome Powell himself was admitting that the job market is probably a lot worse than what it has looked like on the surface in the last couple years.

62200185
>>62184286
>Every job is fake, they will interview for 3 or 4 rounds and still repost the job next week, after ghosting people of course
I've been saying this before but this is the biggest class action law suit in American history waiting to happen. the sheer premeditated and coldhearted and quite honestly treasonous fraud perpetrated on the American labor force is monstrous in size and weight. the amount of time, energy, labor, and national resources wasted by corporate America is of such magnitude it's almost incomprehensible yet it still pales in comparison to the psychological and emotional damages and torture caused by callous greedy corporate monsters to the average American. it's not exaggeration to call the actions of corporations writing out fake job positions never to be filled as plainly one thing, and one thing only: job terrorism. the impact is not imagined, it is too real: innocent, hardworking Americans armed with nothing but a dream of a better life and the pursuit of happiness have committed suicide because of the irresponsible behavior of corporations. corporate America is guilty, guilty of treason, guilty of murder, guilty of the most heinous crimes in American history: any American citizen who has ever been invited to a ghost job interview is according to my estimations owed anywhere between $1-5 million dollars in damages paid. I'm not a lawyer nor is this financial advice nor should anything I write or say ever be taken seriously especially not in this ironic anime board but all I have to say is God Bless America and damn those evil ghost jobs to hell!

62200599
>>62185125
>>62185702
>>62186168
>>62190507
Trump did that

62204337
>>62200068
sure it does, if you networked properly that person wouldnt be bashing your head in. Sad to see you think networking only is one thing, when its a pretty big concept

item.title: Bitcoin or altcoins? — 33 replies — 7.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Bitcoin or altcoins?

Open thread • Replies: 33 • Updated: 2026-05-08 07:38 UTC

OP

If you had to pick, what would you buy right now: BTC or altcoins? I’m not seeing much ROI in alts lately, even utility or memecoins.

Early replies (first 3)

62195293
>>62195275
Bitcoin until 120k

62195300
you can't time the market I thought like >>62195293
last time, bitcoin to 70k and sell half and go into alts and then 2021 alts just fucking died for a year.

The alts that pumped are things that haven't gone parabolic yet, or big projects like Solana with glowie money attached to them. Ones like Link are not going to 1000 regardless of cope.

/biz/ will pretty much only give you bad info too. I've found maybe 3 coins here that were Guaranteed returns, everything else I had to research myself. In fact I remember last year posting proof that CRCL stock was going to 10x on IPO and got XRP schizos fudding me out of taking a life changing position.

I actually hate the fuck out of baggies.

62195373
>>62195293
Why 120k only?? Why not 1M?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62195653
>>62195300
Ok. Just do whatever you wanna do when it comes to your altcoins preferences. That matters.

62195854

BTC and altcoins all good

62195895

62195957

62195988

62196046
>>62195653

62196084

62196112
>>62195373

62197011

62197558
>>62195854

62199876
>>62195895

62200331
>>62197011
Pedo pedo

62200336
>>62195275
Just buy Link and avax

62200341
>>62195988
LINK buy. I will keep telling everyone to buy this

62200348
>>62195275
>Btc
Eth
link
Sui
Sol
Bonk
Neo

Rate my bags

62200350
I will go all in Solana because it the most popular plataform for tokens. If you want to rugpull then you will use Solana System.

Bitcoin can`t 5x in 2 years. There is not enough money in the market for that.

62201146
>>62200350
All in or bust

62201148
>>62200341
Then nerve

62201149
>>62195957
Ironically, eth might be useful if only because jews will push it back to 6k in the next cycle. From a 1500 bottom to 6k, that's 4x in guaranteed returns. if I had 300k in my pocket I'd probably pick eth.

62201348
>>62201149
Let my whale friend pump ETH

62201349
>>62200348
Neo is an old bag kek

62201352
>>62201149
Why eth? Why not btc

62202350
>>62201352
Do you hold BTC? If you’re into it, you can also stake it through protocols that let you keep self-custody.

62202675
>>62195957
Hey Vitalik

62204580
>>62202350
It's no longer possible now and is risky

62204588
but alts usually move hardest once liquidity rotates.

62204590
>>62195275
Depends on your risk tolerance honestly. BTC for stability, alts for asymmetric upside.

62204593
>>62204580
Stop wasting your time on defi shit. Hacks are rampant but if you want to lose money, go all in lmao

62204595
Alts feel dead until they suddenly aren’t

62205030
>>62202350
So far Babylon’s my choice here. I’m not really into wrapped versions, I prefer native BTC exposure.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62200286

Because they wish for the sperm of high quality men and they reside in cities.

62200359

62200377
>>62197690
indiana probably

62200398

62200560

62200576

why do everyone in uscuck behave like nigs? meanwhile asians in japan are cute and not this fucking mentally ill

62201089

It wears you down anon

62202665

62202674

maybe she would finally learn to speak fucking English if we lived in OK

62202683

I thought that was sao paulo

62202687

shes overdosing on BWC - look at that energy

62202752

loud animal sex with her NOW

62202761

>>62200359

62202870
>>62198061

I finished already maybe later

62202923
>>62197777

62202930
>>62202870
post it

62202954
>>62200377
Don't move to Indiana, nothing is there...it's Iowa with extra steps.

62202963
Women are essentially insane. They select for "safety" and that "safety" is never ever safe enough to keep her insane feelings of 'I'm not totally safe' in check.
So you get shit like OPs woman where she could afford to move but her batshit insane womanly demand for a perfect security blanket stops her. It's also generally why you get shit like women being communists so the state's safety net expands infinitely to make her feel safe (she still won't feel safe).

62202987
>>62197690
Ohio.

62203003
>>62202963
The trick is once they have children it keeps them occupied and gives them an outlet for their drive to make things safe.

Unmarried over 24 should be a massive red flag for any woman. It's a sign of bad priorities and ability to plan for the future.

I love Asian women so much

62203228

she's in college and probably living with parents

62203471

62203474

item.title: is zcash superior because it has optional privacy, therefore being able to skim through AML? — 38 replies — 5.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
is zcash superior because it has optional privacy, therefore being able to skim through AML?

Open thread • Replies: 38 • Updated: 2026-05-08 04:57 UTC

OP

it has vastly overtaken monero. but it is not "used" as much as monero

Early replies (first 3)

62194143
>>62193936
>it has vastly overtaken monero.
The last time it has overtaken XMR twitter personalities got paid to shill zec.
>therefore being able to skim through AML?
Depends on how the law is written, definitely a grey area for many AML laws.

62194158
>>62193936
Monero is for midwits

62194187
>>62194158
>Monero is for midwits
true.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62195959
>>62195139
>you guys love zcash
we don't

62196002
Why is Zcash always being shilled by jeets and Nigerians?

62196292
>>62195809
Wow what a profound and technical take

62196355
zooko is a pedofile and zcrash is going to zero

62200065
>>62195139
what desktop infra do you need? ywallet works on my machine

62200161
Truly decentralized CPU only privacy coins are gonna be huge. Grab a bag before they take off.

62200222
No one uses zcash

Monero is the most used crypto on darknet markets. Its the only crypto with product market fit besides bitcoin.

62200292
>>62193936
Optional privacy means any use of privacy will automatically be seen as suspicious.

62200958
>>62193936
zcash is better for speculators
monero is more useful. it's like comparing BTC to ETH

62201103
>>62200958
>zcash is better for speculators
How do you speculate on something with piss-poor adoption prospects?

62201118
>>62200958
>monero is more useful
False. Zcash does Bitcoin and Monero's jobs better than both of them.

62201192
>>62201103
Bigger fool

62203122
The privacy landscape today and its problems:

1) Monero RingCT: the issue: weak privacy, extremely easy to filter out decoys from real spends. The good: proofs verified by your node, low inflation risk

2) Monero FCMP: the issue: optional privacy, weak due to full view keys that can be aggregated to monitor flows in & out of respective wallets. Or they can be flat out leaked through non reproducible wallet implementation backdoors and become a master key that completely deanonymizes the network. Security: high inflation risk because you don't verify transaction proofs at your node, it uses a zero knowledge proof circuit ("FCMP++") and you only verify what the circuit says about the transaction.

3) Zcash: the issue: optional privacy. Mitigation possible by controlling the timing and amounts of the unshielding process. Full view keys of shielded pool wallets could be leaked through non reproducible binaries. Security: high inflation risk because again you cannot verify transaction proofs directly with your node, it uses a zero knowledge proof circuit ("SNARKs") and your node only verified what the SNARK says.

4) Dero: the issue: Captain minted 2M coins for himself in the first year of deployment (2022) when binaries were not reproducible. That's 9% of the supply (9/23). The good: strongest privacy by default, no view keys (nothing to leak), low inflation risk because it uses homomorphic encryption, all transactions are verified directly by your node homomorphically xyou don't trust a binary circuit.

Now pick your poison.

I'd mention other lowcaps but the real choice is between Xmr, Zec and Dero because other privacy coins follow either the Xmr model or the Zec model. For example Salvium is a Monero fork that already uses CARROT for "compliance". ARRR uses Zec's SNARKs but is run by a team that just copies Zec's updates without even understanding the inherent inflation risk. If Arrr is/was ever exploited there will be no way to know, no red flags.

62203128
>>62203122
Grin is similar to Monero but without rings and stealth addresses, it uses UTXO with Pedersen Commitments but since it has no rings no nullifiers are needed because there is no sender privacy (no decoys). Beam similar to Grin, no sender privacy so again very easy to build the transaction graph.

Dero introduces a new standard by using a different primitive (El Gamal) and no utxo model (the only one to do so) which is the strongest option tech wise because it makes it impossible to build a transaction graph.

Xelis stands to Dero like Grin to Monero, it uses Ristretto ElGamal which means no sender/receiver privacy because it's not public key rerandomizable so it's not possible to have decoys. You can tell the exact sender and receiver of each transaction just by looking it up in an explorer. But since it uses HE also in Xelis it's not possible, or at least much harder compared to Cryptonote coins, to build a transaction graph. Still it's more transparent than Dero since you can study the full transaction history of each account and look for behavioral patterns. Ie: if you use Xelis an outsider will know exactly which other accounts/exchanges you sent money to and received money from even though they don't see amounts.

Dero is the only coin with privacy by default, and the most advanced implementation of El Gamal, where you can't build a transaction graph while being able to verify all TX proofs w/ your node directly.

But can you live with the fact that the Captains behind it minted 2.2M coins for themselves after spending the first pre-mine in the 2021 bull? That's the question. That's pure pathological greed on Captain's side. Or, more realistically, "Captains" since Captain is probably a fictitious identity used by devs that contribute to Dero under other pseudonymns to this day.

62203136
>>62203128
OTOH they released the best privacy tech to date by far. Maybe 2.2M is acceptable considering they can't ask for VC funding, have to lay low/stay anonymous and fund themselves somehow? Idk.

What I know is that if I'm looking for privacy, nothing else comes remotely close to Dero tech wise.

62203225
>>62203122
>>62203128
Midwit takes.
Real solution: coinjoin. On Bitcoin.
Also, LN, on Bitcoin.

Sorry, your alt is not needed.

62203313
>>62203225
Sorry, already been evaluated and rejected for sucking.

62203334
>>62203313
>some druggie is afraid of using tech
cool story bro

62203382
>>62203225
>coinjoin and ln
kek le bitshitter cope gtfo nigger

62203402
>>62195101
Druggies are midwits at best. Losers pissing their lives away over plants soaked in acetone.

62203465
>>62203334
>>some druggie is afraid of using tech
More like tech-savvy criminals with everything on the line have found BTC's privacy "solutions" inadequate and unreliable while Monero ticks all the required boxes.

62203635
>>62203465
You would get laughed at so much if you didn't take yourself so seriously. Try leaning into it instead of sneeding over criticism Mr ex XMR general OP

62203740
>>62203635
Non-rebuttal rebuttal.

62203823
>>62203122
sorry what? ZKPs do not cause "inflation risk"

62203853
>>62203740
You've been posting the same shitty takes for the better part of a decade, immune to all arguments. You don't deserve rebuttals at this point, you deserve ridicule.

62203902
>>62203853
Nice try but still a non-rebuttal rebuttal. Because you have no rebuttal. So you try to distract with ad hominems. Keep going.

62204119
>>62203122
>>62203128
>>62203136
>Dero shill

See >>62204099

62204445
>>62203902
My only thing I am arguing is that you're retarded and immune to logic. I reference your long history of being retarded and immune to logic, so that I don't have to slog through another retarded conversation with you. If that's an ad hominem, that's the point. I'm arguing about you, not Monero. Maybe you have trouble distinguishing between the two because it makes up 100% of your personality.

62204821
>>62203402
yes
but the liquidity on DNM is still dependent on those midwits
zcash shielded pool needs a specific wallet with anti-retard infra if it wants to even replace bitcoin on DNMs

62204824
>>62203334
that's about right
state channels have very garbage infra for self custody, most solutions involve routing through custodial as backup
that's too many footguns for DNM guys, you will never see LN adoption on any escrow, save for rare exceptions where two ultra-bitcoin nerds collide

item.title: The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization. — 96 replies — 5.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization.

Open thread • Replies: 96 • Updated: 2026-05-07 17:41 UTC

OP

The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy
Today's debt milestone is not borne from war mobilization. It is the accumulated result of tax cuts without matching reductions in spending, an aging population drawing more heavily on Social Security and Medicare, and years of political leadership on both sides of the aisle that has consistently chosen short-term popularity over long-term solvency.
You fags are cooked (including me)

Early replies (first 3)

62179629
Doesn’t matter, because no one can collect from the US Govt

Refute this without sounding mad

62179630
>>62179622
breaking news: voters want stuff and dont want to pay for it

62179853
>>62179622
AI will fix this

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62192220
>>62188593
Yummy

62193530
>>62179629
Food
>America is food secure
Unti AI agriculture was the most subsidized industry in the country.

62193681
>>62184336
Just re-arrange american healthcare and social security system. Private healthcare and private education are incredibly costly.

62193707
>>62184336
The system more than likely is incapable of being fixed at this point and cutting benefits is political suicide.

62193761
>>62193707
The fix is inflation. They'll keep running it hot to inflate away the debt while forcing bond rates lower artificially.

62194225
>>62179622
Doesn't matter its all fabricated when the fed can print infinitely

62194394
>>62179629
The world stopping to use of the US dollar as the de-facto reserve currency and universal trade tender is not something the US in its current state can survive tho.

Any other country in the world would be facing the greatest hyperinflation event ever seen if they did the shit the US is doing. They're the only things barely preventing it right now.

62194405
>>62184336
you assume things aren't being played out exactly as intended

62197937
>>62179622
me when bi dl trade verbal dom

62200684
>>62179622
>The US national debt is now bigger than the entire US economy

is that bad? Most people have more debt than cash in the bank, not a big deal! Plus, Trump loves debt. Its all part of the plan.

62200692
>>62179928
i'm with this guy. eat shit commies

62200774
>>62200684
The sovereign bond ponzi scheme rolling over depends on the active population out numbering the inactive population that demands monthly checks for doing nothing. The inactive population is outnumbering the active population 20:1. No that's not sustainable anymore. The risk the debt carries is irrational the debt is uncovered, it would need to be defaulted on , respective bought up by a bad bank. The former is the dead of the global ponziconomy the latter is asking for the active population defecting and making the debt even more uncovered

The sovereign bond ponzis are over

62200945
>>62179630
Yeah I don't think anyone voted for bank bailouts, PPP loans, corporate tax breaks or the 1.5 trillion dollar military budget

62200948
>>62200684
>people are the same as national economies
RETARD ALARM

62200952
>>62179928
You dumb fuck the debt isn't something that you take from no country whatsoever the point is nobody is lending you any money and then you uscuck are finished

62200965
>>62180253
nigger if we stop paying our debt the entire world's economy collapses and china becomes the new superpower overnight. going to bomb some shithole is not changing any of that

62200972
>>62180253
yeah sure get usabugs nuked for good retarded fuck

62200980
>>62200945
it's more like the government puts up a facade of employment while taking out 3 trillion every year. just dismantle this unconstitutional ass government

62201141
>>62179622
It’s just time for this country to go and fade away and fall asleep forever, hopefully the death throes of a dying empire will be quick with little collateral dmg

62201178
The amount of debt genuinely doesn’t matter at all because the currency keeps inflating, and the currency keeps inflating because we keep injecting money into the system via govt spending, which gets us further in debt. It’s a cyclical infinite money glitch and nobody will stop it because it’s just the way a system that uses fiat currency works

62201273
>>62201178
If the debt doesn't matter Marx, then why doesn't the gubermint just default on the uncovered ponzi schemes and instead needs to force legislate private actors to buy up the toxic trash bond waste

62201365
>>62201273
because that would destroy the legitimacy of the system and they couldn't keep doing it. why the fuck would they do that?

62201624
>>62179622
They will inflate away the debt while keeping the dollar relevant as long as possible by massive military spending (which accomplishes both goals.) The draft will return while American citizens become increasingly desperate and impoverished and the debt gets inflated into nothing. As foreign demand for dollars collapses they will force 401ks and pension funds to buy them for the greater good, and everyone who doesn't own assets will be wiped out. S&P will hit ATH after ATH while bread becomes $50 a loaf. Finally after WW3 they'll restructure the whole economic system into something else altogether.

62202110
>>62201178
yeah and if the currency inflates it's worth nothing, the us economy crumbles and you fat fucks are going to be paying 20 times more for your burgies

62202185
>>62179629
>americans 2 hours after chinks stop selling to them

62202241
If there was one good thing COVID could have done, then it would have been killing all the old people (ages 65 and up) and the people with comorbidities. Honestly, the decrease in entitlement payments would have really helped the budget for all countries. In the long run, even with the unnecessary lockdowns, the event would have been net positive. Instead we got a faggy lock down and transferred more wealth to the old fags.

62202250
>>62202241
every country got a huge debt because of boomers once again when it was the perfect timing to get rid of them

62202273
>>62202241
That's true but unfortunately most of the developed world is a gerontocracy anon, which is why society got shut down and the youth missed all their developmental milestones for 2 years so the old could feel safer from the flu.

62202596
>>62179928
>nobody has ever invaded the United States.
Ha.

62202612
>>62179928
>Imagine 300 million Americans using guerilla warfare tactics
top lel

item.title: You kids are gonna experience dotcom — 28 replies — 3.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
You kids are gonna experience dotcom

Open thread • Replies: 28 • Updated: 2026-05-08 04:22 UTC

OP

You can thank the grifter in chief for dumping so much liquidity and bullshit tweets for the algos that we are actually going to get THE blowoff top as CPI is set to print 4.5% next week

Couldn’t be a more fitting ending. I mean, how else could you top bankrupting a casino?

Crassus would blush

Early replies (first 3)

62200123
Dotcom and gfc are going to look like a mild correction compared to 60 years of pyramid ponzi scheming on a deflating demographic popping. The bond market is 2 months away from literally dying

62200131
>>62200106
never happening the govt will keep pumping the markets it will never crash like dotcom ever again.

62200139
>>62200131
Aha, that's why the legislator is already so desperate that it forces private companies to buy into the bankrupt sovereign bond ponzi scheme. Lol

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62200179
>>62200106
I've been shorting the market since biden's first and only term. AMA

62200236
>>62200123
As if debt matters anymore. It’s just accounting; doesn’t matter if it’s positive or negative.

62200242
>>62200123
how can I take advantage of this with barely any funds?

62200244
>>62200179
anime girls y/n?

62200265
>>62200236
What do you think is the collateral for the numbers on a screen that is your bank account and 401k?

62200289
>>62200242
Find boomer stocks with low implied volatility on their options. Look at Honda or Siriusxm. You can buy near otm long dated calls for pretty cheap. If you think they v shape recover then your cheap calls go ballistic.

62200345
>>62200289
>Calls
Fucking asshole

62200621
>>62200106
The official response is new paradigm: Waging consumers do not matter anymore, it's only about rich people getting their circular economy. With Palantir, Google, Cloudflare, Criteo and the likes, and AI thought guardrails, the poorscum can be made die off silently within two generations, to make room for a better world of rich people. Remember, Holodomor happened, and commies stayed in power for more than half a century. This time,

62201300
>>62200244
Kirara is the sluttist GoPri and pleases old men for financial gain

62201322
>>62200621
>duuude famines happened 100 years ago so everyone would be okay with a famine today
Zooms don't understand how different the world was 100 years ago

62201334
The AI bubble is like the crypto bubble.
It will continue to make money and be profitable, over evaluated start ups will be destroyed.
Just buy APPL and you will be somewhat shielded from the ai bubble burst

62201342
>>62201334
The underlying is dead. How do you think pension funds are going to try to fulfill their contractual obligations? Yes they have to dump more ponzi papers, mostly shit like Appl and google and other boomer favorites into a market that simply doesn't have the bid to absorb them.

62201986
>>62200106
>This time it's the same
Actually this time it's different

62202002
>>62201334
>appl
retard

62203370
>>62201322
Post covid conditions prove people will accept anything as long as you don't take their phone away from them. Online doomers don't count

62203387
>>62203370
That worked once. And that one time cheat was wasted for a situation that should have been solved by throwing the boomer under the bus and defaulting on the sovereign bond ponzis from the early 10s. Looking at the trust ratings it wont work a second time, certainly not in the matter of less than a decade.

Also it was a giant failure. Non of the draconian socialist authoritarian surveillance methods applied were able to be carried over and now the population is aware of it. Boomer polity fucked up and is now stuck between a rock and a hard place when it most matters between 2026 and 2029

62203407
Everyone says we will experience a crash, which means we won't

62203409
>>62203387
every attempt at a bail out, or the Central bank trying to go QE and YCC is going to be honored with the defection of the active population, and if really unlucky, occupy wallstreet is going to look like a hippy camp compared to what happens next

62203423
>>62203407
retard, depite the nominal value "ATHs" those that bought the post 2022 "dips" are at a loss compared to 2019 if taking hyper monetary inflation into account. The SPX might look like its at an ATH, in real terms its under 2019 levels

62203456
>>62203407
who is everyone? you mean the perma bobos?
normies and retail are exposed to tech
I even asked normie EU friends

62204335
>>62200106
>>62200123
t. retards with no source

62204348
>>62200106
how did things end for the Luddites?

62204753
>>62204335
the source is the foundational collateral of 60 years of ponzi scheming. Rolling over and inflating uncovered generational public debt, leveraging it, wasting it, making unsustainable promises, legislating them into entitlements and expecting that the boomer bulge switching from net positive cash inflow to net negative cash outflow from the ponzis is not going to have an effect of incentive structure as changing the rationality of cooperation by the active population to rational defection from the social and financial state.

In summary the source is simple arithmetic and game theory

62204763
>>62204753
and the effects are already observable. Decline of paying into socialist pension ponzi schemes, reducing work hours below taxable income and removing cashflow the state and its creditors that bought up the trash bonds expected when they emitted respective bought the ponzis, retreatment into informal economic structures and open defiance of legislated paper laws.

The world is due to 60 years of boomer policy failures on a straight path to become a failed state on a global scale; and the chance to make a clean cut by defaulting on the debt as throwing the boomer under the bus was missed and instead a 20th century retard bail out attempt was performed that decayed trust harder than anything else before observed in human governance history.

The choice was deflate or die, the polity for shirt term profits chose to die

62204772
I market has stayed irrational for far longer than I can remain solvent. I just DCA now, i've given up.
